```diff
         bytes4(keccak256("recordSettlementQueued(address,address,address,uint256,uint256)"));
     bytes4 internal constant RECORD_SETTLEMENT_ANNULLED_SELECTOR =
         bytes4(keccak256("recordSettlementAnnulled(address,address,address,uint256,uint256)"));
     bytes4 internal constant RECORD_SETTLEMENT_PROCESSED_SELECTOR =
         bytes4(keccak256("recordSettlementProcessed(address,address,address,uint256,uint256,uint256)"));
     bytes4 internal constant RECORD_SETTLEMENT_FAILED_SELECTOR =
+        // TODO: Update to include `bytes` reason in the function selector for HubCallback.
         bytes4(keccak256("recordSettlementFailed(address,address,address,uint256,uint256)"));
     bytes4 internal constant PROCESS_SETTLEMENTS_SELECTOR =
         bytes4(keccak256("processSettlements(address,address[],address[],uint256[])"));
     bytes4 internal constant TRIGGER_MORE_LIQUIDITY_AVAILABLE_SELECTOR =
         bytes4(keccak256("triggerMoreLiquidityAvailable(address,address,uint256)"));
 }
```

### 10. [Informational] Misleading NatSpec for Currency parameters in IVTSCurrencyDelta.getUnderlyingDeltaPair causes runtime reverts for incorrect inputs

#### Status

Review status: Unresolved
Remediation status: Unremediated
Remediation note: Created by pipeline analysis

#### Description

[IVTSCurrencyDelta.getUnderlyingDeltaPair](https://github.com/usherlabs/fiet-protocol/blob/f5235186da277999275f3a0a6ae3cadd91ddf1e8/contracts/evm/src/interfaces/IVTSCurrencyDelta.sol#L70) documents currency0 and currency1 as “(address)” while the function expects LCC currencies (Uniswap v4 Currency). The implementation casts inputs to ILCC and [calls underlying()](https://github.com/usherlabs/fiet-protocol/blob/f5235186da277999275f3a0a6ae3cadd91ddf1e8/contracts/evm/src/interfaces/ILCC.sol#L14), so passing underlyings, EOAs, or non-LCC tokens causes a revert. This is a documentation-driven integration pitfall leading to view-call failures and UX/liveness issues, not fund loss.

The interface [IVTSCurrencyDelta.getUnderlyingDeltaPair](https://github.com/usherlabs/fiet-protocol/blob/f5235186da277999275f3a0a6ae3cadd91ddf1e8/contracts/evm/src/interfaces/IVTSCurrencyDelta.sol#L70) takes Currency types but its NatSpec labels [currency0 and currency1 as “(address)”](https://github.com/usherlabs/fiet-protocol/blob/f5235186da277999275f3a0a6ae3cadd91ddf1e8/contracts/evm/src/interfaces/IVTSCurrencyDelta.sol#L66-L67). The implementation immediately [converts these to underlying currencies](https://github.com/usherlabs/fiet-protocol/blob/f5235186da277999275f3a0a6ae3cadd91ddf1e8/contracts/evm/src/libraries/OwnerCurrencyDelta.sol#L88-L91) via [OwnerCurrencyDelta.lccToUnderlyingCurrency](https://github.com/usherlabs/fiet-protocol/blob/f5235186da277999275f3a0a6ae3cadd91ddf1e8/contracts/evm/src/libraries/OwnerCurrencyDelta.sol#L75-L77), which casts the input to ILCC and [calls underlying()](https://github.com/usherlabs/fiet-protocol/blob/f5235186da277999275f3a0a6ae3cadd91ddf1e8/contracts/evm/src/interfaces/ILCC.sol#L14). If an integrator passes underlying token addresses, EOAs, or non-LCC contracts, the underlying() call will revert due to missing function/returndata. The function is external view and does not mutate state; thus, failures are confined to the caller’s transaction or off-chain queries. [Internal protocol flows](https://github.com/usherlabs/fiet-protocol/blob/f5235186da277999275f3a0a6ae3cadd91ddf1e8/contracts/evm/src/libraries/VTSLifecycleLinkedLib.sol#L250) [pass correct LCC currencies (e.g., poolKey.currency0/1)](https://github.com/usherlabs/fiet-protocol/blob/f5235186da277999275f3a0a6ae3cadd91ddf1e8/contracts/evm/src/libraries/VTSPositionMMOpsLib.sol#L199), so core protocol safety is unaffected. The issue is a documentation inconsistency that can cause integration/runtime reverts and degraded UX/liveness for callers relying on the ambiguous NatSpec.

#### Severity

**Impact Explanation:** [Informational] The issue is a read-path/view-only documentation discrepancy causing integration reverts and UX/liveness friction for callers; it does not affect protocol state, funds, or invariants.

**Likelihood Explanation:** [Low] All scenarios rely on integrator/operator misuse or misinterpretation of the NatSpec; while plausible, it is still a user-side mistake.

#### Exploitation

## Exploitation Scenarios

### Scenario 1

An on-chain integrator contract queries getUnderlyingDeltaPair before executing an action and, due to the “(address)” NatSpec, passes underlying token addresses (e.g., WETH, USDC). The call reverts at ILCC.underlying(), causing the entire transaction/batch to revert and waste gas.

#### Preconditions / Assumptions

- (a). Integrator relies on NatSpec labeling currency0/1 as “(address)” and assumes underlyings are acceptable
- (b). Integrator passes underlying token addresses instead of LCC addresses
- (c). getUnderlyingDeltaPair is called on-chain inside a transactional flow
- (d). The provided addresses do not implement ILCC.underlying()

### Scenario 2

An off-chain backend service exposes an API to fetch underlying deltas and, based on the ambiguous NatSpec, forwards underlyings or even an EOA to getUnderlyingDeltaPair. The call reverts when decoding underlying(), leading to API 500s or failed multicall aggregations and degraded monitoring/UX.

#### Preconditions / Assumptions

- (a). Backend service uses getUnderlyingDeltaPair to serve an API
- (b). Service or frontend/config supplies underlyings or EOA due to ambiguous NatSpec
- (c). The provided address does not implement ILCC.underlying()
- (d). Service treats revert as request failure, breaking aggregation or returning 500s

### Scenario 3

A frontend performs a multicall to fetch metrics including getUnderlyingDeltaPair but supplies underlyings due to the NatSpec wording. The multicall reverts, blocking UI data display and potentially causing misplanned user transactions that later revert and waste gas.

#### Preconditions / Assumptions

- (a). Frontend builds a multicall that includes getUnderlyingDeltaPair
- (b). Dev passes underlyings based on NatSpec wording
- (c). Multicall reverts on missing ILCC.underlying(), blocking UI or leading to misplanning
